activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Use numerical skills in different contexts
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explain the basic concepts of Mathematics (Meaning of mathematics, branches of mathematics, relationship between mathematics and other subjects, importance of mathematics)
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Guide students to discuss meaning of mathematics and branches of mathematics
Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on importance of mathematics in daily life
Jigsaw: Guide students to explain the relationship of mathematics with other disciplines
activities.assessment_criteria: The basic concept of mathematics are clearly explained
suggested_resources: Charts of relationships between mathematics and other fields, real life objects, Math Games and Apps, Educational channels

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Use numerical skills in different contexts
activities.learning_activity: (b) Explain the concept of rational, irrational, and real numbers
suggested_methods: Observation: Provide students with visual aids like bars/strips or circles and guide them to cut the aids in small equal parts. Relate their responses to the meaning of fractions

Problem solving: Organise students in manageable groups and assign each group word problems that require students to apply their knowledge of fractions, decimals and percentages to real word scenarios. Guide them to present the results and relate their findings with rational numbers
Exploration: Guide students to relate real numbers to real-life situations, for example, money and measurement
activities.assessment_criteria: Rational, irrational, and real numbers are well explained
suggested_resources: Calculator, Charts of real numbers, ruler, tape measure, graph papers, number line chart

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Use numerical skills in different contexts
activities.learning_activity: (b) Convert repeating/recurring decimals into fractions and vice versa
suggested_methods: Problem solving: Provide students with examples of decimals and guide them to identify repeating/recurring decimals, and convert repeating/recurring decimals into fractions and vice versa and share with the rest of the class through presentations
activities.assessment_criteria: Repeating/recurring decimals are correctly converted into fractions
suggested_resources: Calculator, Charts of real numbers, ruler, tape measure, graph papers

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Use numerical skills in different contexts
activities.learning_activity: (c) Represent rational numbers on a number line
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Organise students in manageable groups to draw number lines. Guide them to discuss steps of representing rational numbers on a number line and share their results with the rest of the class through presentations
Problem solving: Guide students to suggest real-life problems involving rational numbers. Ask them to locate points on the number line and perform calculations based on the given scenario
activities.assessment_criteria: Rational numbers are well represented on the number line

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Use numerical skills in different contexts
activities.learning_activity: (d) Explain the concept of inequalities and absolute values of real numbers
suggested_methods: Intuitive approach: Guide students visual aids such as number line charts and graphing tools to explore the basic concepts of inequalities and absolute values of real numbers.
activities.assessment_criteria: Absolute values and inequalities in real numbers are properly determined

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Use numerical skills in different contexts
activities.learning_activity: (e) Describe the importance of numbers in real-life situations
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide the students to describe the importance of numbers and apply numbers in real-life situations
activities.assessment_criteria: Importance of numbers are well described

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Use ratios and proportions in daily life
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept of ratios and proportions
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss and come up with real-life scenarios involving the concept of ratios and proportions. Share with the rest of the class through presentations
Jigsaw: Guide students to explore how other subjects relate to concept of ratios and proportions
activities.assessment_criteria: Ratios and proportions are clearly explained
suggested_resources: Money, charts of real-life scenarios, interest calculators, and games real objects, and bank statements

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Use ratios and proportions in daily life
activities.learning_activity: (b) Solve ratio and proportion problems
suggested_methods: Think-ink-pair-share: Guide students to solve ratio and proportion problems and share the results with classmates
activities.assessment_criteria: Problems on ratios and proportions are correctly solved
suggested_resources: Calculator, Charts of real numbers, ruler, tape measure, graph papers, number line chart

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept of approximations (rounding off, significant figures, and decimal places)
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: In manageable groups guide Students to explore the concept of approximations and share their findings with other groups.
Think-ink-pair-share: Guide students to pair up to discuss their ideas, thereafter, some of the pairs share their ideas with the whole class
activities.assessment_criteria: Approximations are clearly explained
suggested_resources: Charts of a number line, estimation jars, estimation games and applications

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
activities.learning_activity: (b) Round off numbers and estimate values of expressions
suggested_methods: Group discussion: In manageable groups, guide students to round off numbers and estimate values of expressions and share their findings
activities.assessment_criteria: Numbers are rounded off and values of expressions are correctly estimated

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
activities.learning_activity: (c) Approximate numbers to the required significant figures and decimal places
suggested_methods: Jigsaw: Guide students to use real-life examples to approximate numbers to the required significant figures and decimal places
Problem solving: Assess the accuracy of answers provided by students when approximating numbers and provide them with feedback
activities.assessment_criteria: Numbers are approximated to required significant figures and decimal places correctly

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
activities.learning_activity: (d) Use approximations in computations and measurements of quantities in various contexts
suggested_methods: Jigsaw: Organise students in manageable groups to discover the use of approximations in computations and measurements of quantities in various contexts
activities.assessment_criteria: Approximations are correctly applied in computations and measurements.

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explore the basic tenets of algebra (algebraic expressions and equations, linear simultaneous equations of two unknowns, inequalities in one unknown)
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Organize students in manageable groups, guide them to discuss algebraic expressions and equations
Visual representations: Provide learners with visual aids such as diagrams, charts, and graphs to illustrate the graphs of linear equations
activities.assessment_criteria: The basic tenets of algebra are correctly explored
suggested_resources: Beam balance, mathematical software such as MATLAB, and GeoGebra, real objects, Algebra animations

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: (b) Use algebraic expressions to model situations (word problems into algebraic expressions and equations)
suggested_methods: Problem solving: Incorporate word problems that require students to translate verbal descriptions into algebraic expressions and equations. Guide them to identify key words that indicate mathematical operations
activities.assessment_criteria: Algebraic expressions are applied to model situations correctly
suggested_resources: Beam balance, mathematical software such as MATLAB, and GeoGebra, real objects, Algebra animations

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: (c) Solve simultaneous equations using substitution and elimination methods
suggested_methods: Think-ink-pair-share: Guide students in pair to solve simultaneous equations using substitution and elimination methods Thereafter, allow some of the pairs to share their results with the whole class
activities.assessment_criteria: Simultaneous linear equations are solved correctly

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: (d) Solve inequalities in one unknown
suggested_methods: Group discussion: In manageable groups, guide the students to solve inequalities in one unknown and present results for common understanding
activities.assessment_criteria: Inequalities are solved properly
suggested_resources: Number line chart, graphing calculator, real objects

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability and statistics
activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry and vectors skills in daily life
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explore the basic tenets of coordinate geometry (gradient and equations of a straight line, graphs of linear equations)
suggested_methods: Collaborative learning: Guide students to use visual aids, such as graphs and coordinate planes to establish the relationship between coordinates and geometrical shapes
activities.assessment_criteria: Basic tenets of coordinate geometry are explored
suggested_resources: Mathematical software such as MATLAB, and GeoGebra, Maple, Mathematica, graph papers

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability and statistics
activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry and vectors skills in daily life
activities.learning_activity: (b) Find the gradient/slope of a line
suggested_methods: Problem solving: In manageable groups, guide the students to determine the slope of a given line
activities.assessment_criteria: Gradient of line is found properly

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability and statistics
activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry and vectors skills in daily life
activities.learning_activity: (c) Determine the equation of a straight line and draw its graph
suggested_methods: Visual representation: Utilize visual aids like diagrams and charts to illustrate the concept of a straight line visually. Provide examples of different types of straight lines and their corresponding equations and draw their graphs
activities.assessment_criteria: Equation of straight line is determined and its graph is properly drawn

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability and statistics
activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry and vectors skills in daily life
activities.learning_activity: (d) Solve linear simultaneous equations graphically
suggested_methods: Visual representation: Guide students to use visual aids such as a coordinate plane, graph paper, or interactive whiteboard to plot points, draw lines, and interpret the graph
Group discussion: In manageable groups, guide the students to solve linear simultaneous equations graphically
activities.assessment_criteria: Simultaneous equations are correctly solved graphically

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability and statistics
activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry and vectors skills in daily life
activities.learning_activity: (e) Use mathematical software to solve and draw graphs of simultaneous equations
suggested_methods: Use of technology: Guide students to use mathematical software such as Excel, GeoGebra, Mathematica, MATLAB to solve and draw graphs of simultaneous equations.
Problem solving: In manageable groups, provide students with plenty examples and exercise to solve and draw graphs of simultaneous equations using mathematical software
activities.assessment_criteria: Simultaneous equations are solved and drawn correctly

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Use rates and variations in different contexts
activities.learning_activity: (a) Describe the concepts of rates and variations
suggested_methods: Exploration: Organise students in manageable groups and guide them to explore real-life problems related to the concepts of rates and variations
activities.assessment_criteria: Concepts of rates and variations are well described

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Use rates and variations in different contexts
activities.learning_activity: (b) Solve problems on rates and variations
suggested_methods: Group discussion: In manageable groups, guide students to solve problems involving rates and variations
activities.assessment_criteria: Problems on rates and variations are correctly solved

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
activities.learning_activity: (a) Describe the concepts of geometry (similarities and congruence)
suggested_methods: Exploration: Guide the students to explore shapes and identify congruent and similar figures through direct comparison

Group discussion: In manageable groups, guide students to discuss the concepts of similarities and congruences through hands-on activities that involve physical manipulatives, such as pattern blocks, tangrams or geoboards
activities.assessment_criteria: Concepts of geometry are well described

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
activities.learning_activity: (b) Recognize properties of similar triangles
suggested_methods: Visual representation: Guide students to use pictures, charts, diagrams, and models to recognize properties of similar triangles
Jigsaw: Guide students to constructs and manipulate similar and congruent triangles and associate them to real-life applications
Exploration: Guide students to connect similar triangles to real world applications
activities.assessment_criteria: Properties of similar triangles are clearly recognized

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
activities.learning_activity: (c) Explain postulates, proofs, and theorems of congruent triangles
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Guide students to discuss postulates, proofs, and theorems of congruent triangles
activities.assessment_criteria: Postulates, proofs, and theorems of congruent triangles are clearly explained

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explore the basic tenets of algebra (binary operations, quadratic expressions and equations, radicals, exponents, and logarithms)
suggested_methods: Jigsaw: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss the concepts of algebra (binary operations, quadratic expressions and equations, radicals, exponents, and logarithms)
Graphical methods: Organize students in manageable groups and guide them to use graphs to solve quadratic equations
Use of technology: Guide students to use technology such as Excel, GeoGebra, Microsoft Mathematics, and MATLAB to solve quadratic equations
Problems solving: In manageable groups, guide students to apply binary operations to simplify quadratic expressions
activities.assessment_criteria: Basic tenets of algebra are explored correctly
suggested_resources: Mathematical software such as GeoGebra, MATLAB, Maple, Mathematica, graphing calculators

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: (b) Solve quadratic equations by using different methods (factorisation, Completing the square, and quadratic formula)
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Guide students through solving quadratic equations by using factorisation, completing the square, and quadratic formula
activities.assessment_criteria: Quadratic equations are solved correctly

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: (c) Identify and use laws of exponents involving positive, negative, and zero exponents (multiplication law, division law, power law, and zero index)
suggested_methods: Group discussion: In manageable groups, guide students to identify and discuss multiplication law, division law, power law, and zero index
Problem solving: Guide students to discuss and use laws of exponents involving positive, negative, and zero exponents to simplify and solve exponential equations
activities.assessment_criteria: Laws of exponents are clearly identified and used correctly

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: (d) Write numbers in standard form
suggested_methods: Group discussion: In manageable groups, guide students to discuss and write numbers in standard form
activities.assessment_criteria: Numbers are correctly written in standard form

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: (e) Use laws of logarithms to solve problems
suggested_methods: Interactive discussion: Guide students to discuss and use laws of logarithms to solve problems involving logarithms
activities.assessment_criteria: Laws of logarithms are used to solve problems correctly

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: (f) Perform operations on radicals and rationalize the denominators
suggested_methods: Problem solving: Guide students to discuss and perform operations on radicals and rationalize the denominators
activities.assessment_criteria: Operations on radicals and denominators are clearly performed

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.3 Use sets, sequences and series in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explore the basic tenets of sets (types of sets, subsets, operation with sets, and Venn diagrams of two sets)
suggested_methods: Exploration: Organise students in manageable groups to explore the concepts of sets, subsets, operations of sets, and Venn diagram of two sets
Jigsaw: Organise students in manageable groups to illustrate concepts of sets, subsets, operations of sets, and Venn diagrams of two sets using real-life examples
activities.assessment_criteria: Basic tenets of sets are clearly explored

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.3 Use sets, sequences and series in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: (b) Distinguish among different types of sets (universal set, equal sets, empty/null set, finite and infinite sets, equivalent sets, and disjoint sets)
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss the different types of sets
activities.assessment_criteria: Different types of sets are clearly distinguished

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.3 Use sets, sequences and series in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: (c) Compare sets (subsets and universal sets)
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Organise students in manageable groups to compare subsets and universal sets
activities.assessment_criteria: Sets are clearly compared

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.3 Use sets, sequences and series in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: (d) Perform operations with sets (union, intersection, and complement of a set)
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Organise students in manageable groups to perform operations with sets
activities.assessment_criteria: Operations with sets are correctly performed

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.3 Use sets, sequences and series in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: (e) Represent two sets in a Venn diagram
suggested_methods: Visual representation: Guide students to use charts and tables to represent two sets in a Venn diagram
activities.assessment_criteria: Sets in a Venn diagram are correctly represented

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: 2.3 Use sets, sequences and series in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: (f) Find the number of elements in a set
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss and determine the number of elements in a set
activities.assessment_criteria: Number of elements in a set are correctly found

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability, and statistics
activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry and vectors skills in daily life
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explore the basic tenets of trigonometry (trigonometric ratios, angles of elevation and depression)
suggested_methods: Exploration: Organise students in manageable groups to explore the concepts of trigonometric ratios, angles of elevation and depression using real-life examples
activities.assessment_criteria: The basic tenets of trigonometry are clearly explored
suggested_resources: Mathematical software such as GeoGebra, MATLAB, Maple, Mathematica, graph paper, mathematical set

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability, and statistics
activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry and vectors skills in daily life
activities.learning_activity: (b) Determine trigonometric ratios of angles and special angles
suggested_methods: Group discussion: In manageable groups, guide students to discuss and determine trigonometric ratios of angles and special angles
activities.assessment_criteria: Trigonometric ratios of angles and special angles are clearly determined

activities.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability, and statistics
activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry and vectors skills in daily life
activities.learning_activity: (c) Calculate angles of elevation and depression
suggested_methods: Jigsaw: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss and calculate angles of elevation and depression
activities.assessment_criteria: Angles of elevation and depression are calculated correctly

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
activities.learning_activity: Describe the concepts of relations and functions (linear and quadratic) (types of relations, domain and range of relations, graphs of relations and functions, inverse of relations, and functions)
suggested_methods: Collaborative learning: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss the concepts of relations, functions, types of relations and functions, and link between relations and functions
Exploration: Organise students in manageable groups to explore the concept of relations of family members and price of different commodities in different businesses
Visual representation: Guide students to use graphs, charts, and tables to discuss mappings of two sets
Project: In small groups, guide students to visit nearby shops and relate prices of different commodities with the concept of domain and range of functions
activities.assessment_criteria: Concepts of relations and functions are clearly described
suggested_resources: Mathematical software such as Mathematica, GeoGebra

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
activities.learning_activity: Find the domain and range of relations and functions
suggested_methods: Jigsaw: Organise students in manageable groups and guide them to determine domain and range from graphs, charts, and tables of relations and functions
activities.assessment_criteria: Domain and range of relations and functions are clearly described
suggested_resources: Mathematical software such as Mathematica, GeoGebra

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
activities.learning_activity: Find the inverses of relations and functions
suggested_methods: Problem solving: Equip students with appropriate materials on relations and functions. Guide them to discuss the provided questions on inverses of relations and functions and present their findings
activities.assessment_criteria: The inverse of relations and functions are correctly found
suggested_resources: Mathematical software such as Mathematica, GeoGebra

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
activities.learning_activity: Draw graphs of relations and functions
suggested_methods: Visual representation: In manageable groups guide students to use visual aid to draw graphs of relations and functions
Use of technology: Guide students to use technology such as graphing calculators, and software such as MATLAB to draw graphs of relations and functions
activities.assessment_criteria: The graphs of relations and functions are correctly drawn
suggested_resources: Mathematical software such as Mathematica, GeoGebra

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: Explore the basic tenets of algebra (linear programming: constraints, objective functions, and optimal solution)
suggested_methods: Jigsaw: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss the concepts of linear programming, constraints, objective functions, and optimal solutions of linear programming using real-life examples
Graphical methods: In manageable groups, guide students to use graphs to determine optimal solutions of linear programming problems with two variables
Use of technology: Guide students to use technology such as Excel and MATLAB to determine the optimal solutions of linear programming problems with two variables
Problems solving: In manageable groups, guide students to find optimal solutions of linear programming problems
activities.assessment_criteria: The concepts of linear programming are clearly explored
suggested_resources: Mathematical software such as Mathematica, GeoGebra

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: Use sets, sequences and series in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: Explore the basic tenets of sequences and series (Arithmetic progression AP , Geometric progression GP)
suggested_methods: Exploration: Guide students to explore the concepts of sequences and series
Visual representation: Guide students to use visual aids such as charts, diagrams, graphs, and real objects to discuss the concepts of Arithmetic Progression (AP) and Geometric Progression (GP)
activities.assessment_criteria: The concepts of sequences and series are clearly explored
suggested_resources: sequence and series games and applications

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: Use sets, sequences and series in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: Find the general term for AP and GP and use them to derive formulae for the sums of APs and GPs
suggested_methods: Exploration: Organise students in manageable groups to discover and derive the nth terms of AP and GP
Group discussion: In manageable groups, guide students to determine the nth terms of APs and GPs
activities.assessment_criteria: The formulae for the sum of APs and GPs are correctly derived
suggested_resources: sequence and series games and applications

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: Use sets, sequences and series in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: Calculate arithmetic mean, geometric mean, and compound interest
suggested_methods: Exploration: Organise students in manageable groups to explore the concepts of arithmetic mean, geometric mean, and compound interest
Think-ink-pair-share: In manageable groups, instruct students to discuss and apply the concepts of arithmetic mean, geometric mean, and compound interest in their real-life situation
Interactive discussion: Guide students to apply the concept of compound interest in real-life situations, such as calculating the total cost of loan or the return on an investment over time
activities.assessment_criteria: The arithmetic mean, geometric mean, and compound interest are correctly found
suggested_resources: sequence and series games and applications

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability and statistics
activities.specific_competence: Use basic skills of circles in daily life
activities.learning_activity: Explore the basic tenets of a circle (angle properties, theorems, tangents, chords and radians)
suggested_methods: Think- ink-pair-share: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss the concepts of angle properties of a circle, tangent, theorems, chords, and radians
Scenario: Provide a scenario that shows the application of circles. Allow learners to discuss, make follow up and provide feedback
Visual representation: Guide students to use visual aids such as diagrams, pictures, and animations to understand the concepts of angle properties of a circle, tangent, theorems, chords, and radians
activities.assessment_criteria: The concept of circle is clearly explored

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: Explore the basic tenets of matrices ( 2×2 matrices: operations, determinant, inverse, and transformations)
suggested_methods: Demonstration: Demonstrate the concept of matrices using real-life examples
Visual representation: Guide students to use graphs, charts, and tables to explain the concept of matrices
activities.assessment_criteria: The basic tenets of algebra are explored clearly

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
activities.specific_competence: Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: Apply matrices to solve simultaneous equations of two unknowns (matrix inversion method and Cramer’ s rule)
suggested_methods: Questions and answers: Organise students in manageable groups to determine the solution of simultaneous equations of two unknowns using matrix inversion method and Cramer’s rule

Use of technology: Guide students to use technology such as graphing calculators, and software such as Excel, Microsoft Mathematics, and MATLAB to determine the solutions of simultaneous equations with two unknowns using matrix inversion method and Cramer’s rule
Problem solving: In manageable groups, provide students with examples to practice basic operations on matrices, determinants, inverses, and transformations on matrices
activities.assessment_criteria: Matrices are clearly applied to solve simultaneous equations of two unknowns

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability, and statistics
activities.specific_competence: Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry, and vectors skills in daily life
activities.learning_activity: Explore the basic tenets of coordinate geometry (midpoint of a line segment, distance between two points on a line, parallel, and perpendicular lines)
suggested_methods: Demonstration: Demonstrate the concepts of midpoint of a line segment, distance between two points on a line, parallel lines, and perpendicular lines using real-life problems
Problem solving: Organise students in manageable groups to use real world examples to find the midpoint of a line segment, distance between two points on a line, parallel, and perpendicular lines

Visual representation: Guide students to use graphs, charts, and real objects to explain the concepts of midpoint of a line segment, distance between two points on a line, parallel lines, and perpendicular lines
activities.assessment_criteria: The basic tenets of coordinate geometry are well explored
suggested_resources: Mathematical software such as GeoGebra, Mathematica, MATLAB, Maple

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability, and statistics
activities.specific_competence: Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry, and vectors skills in daily life
activities.learning_activity: Apply sine and cosine rules to find distances or angles of elevation
suggested_methods: Exploration: Guide students to explore the application of sine and cosine rules in finding distances or angles of elevation
Use of technology: Guide students to use technology such as graphing calculators, and software such as Excel and MATLAB to apply sine and cosine rules to find distances or angles of elevation
Problem solving: In manageable groups, provide students with questions to practice on how to apply the sine and cosine rules to determine the distance or angles of elevation
activities.assessment_criteria: The sine and cosine rules are clearly applied to find distances and angles of elevation

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability, and statistics
activities.specific_competence: Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry, and vectors skills in daily life
activities.learning_activity: Derive and use compound angles to solve problems
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Organise students in manageable groups to derive and use compound angles to solve problems
Use of technology: Guide students to use technology such as graphing calculators and compound angles to solve problems
Jigsaw: In manageable groups, provide students with assignment to solve problems
activities.assessment_criteria: The compound angles are clearly derived and used to solve problems

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability, and statistics
activities.specific_competence: Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry, and vectors skills in daily life
activities.learning_activity: Explore the basic tenets of vectors (displacement and position vectors, magnitude and direction, sum and differences, multiplication of vectors by a scalar)
suggested_methods: Exploration: Encourage students individually or in groups to explore the basic tenets of vectors through visual representations, problem solving and its applications in real life

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability, and statistics
activities.specific_competence: Use probability in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: Explore the basic tenets of probability of two events (probability of an event, mutually exclusive events, dependent events, combined events using tree diagrams, tables and formulae)
suggested_methods: Visual representation: Use interactive tools to demonstrate concept of probability of an event
Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss and determine probability of an event, dependent event, combined events using tree diagram, tables and formulae
activities.assessment_criteria: The basic tenets of probability are well explored

activities.main_competence: Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability, and statistics
activities.specific_competence: Use statistics in problem solving
activities.learning_activity: Explore the basic tenets of statistics (frequency distribution, measures of central tendency, histogram, frequency polygon, and cumulative frequency curve ‘ogive’)
suggested_methods: Demonstration: Using real-life examples, guide the students to describe and demonstrate the concepts of frequency distribution and measure of central tendency
Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss and determine the measures of central tendency, draw histogram, frequency polygon and cumulative frequency curve (ogive)
Exploration: Guide students in groups to explore the basic tenets of statistics through the use of technology, visual aids such as charts of data and graphs of real life scenarios
activities.assessment_criteria: The basic tenets of statistics are explored clearly
